zh.lifeSummary: 當Maria Di Torres於1181年出生在義大利薩德尼亞洛古多洛時，她的父親Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres54歲，母親Spella23歲。她和Boniface of Saluzzo至少有1個兒子。她去世於1264年在義大利皮蒙特辛薩盧左享年83歲，埋葬在義大利皮蒙特辛雷福洛阿巴其亞 桑塔 瑪麗亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maria Di Torres于1181年出生在意大利薩德尼亞洛古多洛时，她的父亲Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres54岁，母亲Spella23岁。她和Boniface of Saluzzo至少有1个儿子。她去世于1264年在意大利皮蒙特辛薩盧左享年83岁，埋葬在意大利皮蒙特辛雷福洛阿巴其亞 桑塔 瑪麗亞。

nameMeanings.name: Di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Torres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Raul, Rafael.  Portuguese Ligia, Anatolio, Catarina, Paulo, Albeiro, Gonsalo, Joaquim, Lidio, Omero, Wenceslao.

Galician, Catalan, Spanish, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Torres, all named with the plural of torre ‘tower’ (see Torre ). As a Jewish name, it was often adopted at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics. Compare De Torres .
Italian: habitational name from Torres in Belluno or Porto Torres in Sassari. In southern Italy the surname is sometimes of Spanish origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Torrez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Carlos, Jesus, Armando, Raul, Ruben, Alfredo, Alvaro, Fernando.

variants.name: Torre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmelo, Vito, Antonio, Carmine, Pietro, Sal, Sebastiano, Agostino, Bartolo.  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Carlos, Julio, Alvaro, Eduardo, Elvira, Ernesto, Fernando, Gregorio, Guillermo, Jorge.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived near a tower, torre (from Latin turris), usually a defensive fortification or watchtower, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, as for example Torre Annunziata or Torre del Greco in Naples province, Torre de’ Passeri (Pescara, Abruzzo), or Torre di Ruggiero (Catanzaro, Calabria). Compare La Torre .

Galician, Catalan, Aragonese, Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from torre ‘(watch) tower’ (of the same etymology as 1), or a habitational name from any of numerous places called (La) Torre in Spain and Portugal. Compare Spanish De la Torre and La Torre .

nameMeanings.name: Maria
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Maria Di Torres was born in 1181, in Logudoro, Sardinia, Italy, her father, Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, was 54 and her mother, Spella, was 23. She had at least 1 son with Boniface of Saluzzo. She died in 1264, in Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy, at the age of 83, and was buried in Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maria Di Torres est née en 1181, à Logudoro, Sardegna, Italie, son père, Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Spella, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Boniface of Saluzzo. Elle est décédée en 1264, à Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italie, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maria Di Torres 1181 in Logudoro, Sardinien, Italien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres 54 und ihre Mutter Spella 23. Sie hatte mit Boniface of Saluzzo mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1264 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italien und wurde in Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maria Di Torres è nata il nel 1181 nel Logudoro, Sardegna, Italia, sua padre, Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, aveva 54 anni e sua madre, Spella, aveva 23 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Boniface of Saluzzo. Morì nel 1264, nel Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolta a Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia.

ja.lifeSummary: Maria Di Torresが1181年にイタリアサルデグナログドロで生まれた時，父Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torresは54歳，母Spellaは23歳であった。彼女はBoniface of Saluzzoとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1264年にイタリアピエモントクウネオサルズゾで83歳で死去し，イタリアピエモントクウネオレベロアッバズア サンタ マリアにて埋葬された。

ko.Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Pie: 이탈리아 피몽 쿤오 러에벨로 애브바지아 산타 마이아
ko.lifeSummary: Maria Di Torres(이)가 1181년 이탈리아 사르데그나 로구도로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Spella(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Boniface of Saluzzo(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1264년 이탈리아 피몽 쿤오 살루즈오에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 피몽 쿤오 러에벨로 애브바지아 산타 마이아에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maria Di Torres nasceu em 1181, em Logudoro, Sardegna, Itália, seu pai, Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Spella, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Boniface of Saluzzo. Ela faleceu em 1264, em Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Itália, com 83 anos, e foi sepultada em Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Piemonte, Itália.

ru.Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Pie: эббазя санта маря, ривеллоу, кунео, пиемонт, Италия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maria Di Torres родилась в 1181 г. в местности логудоро, сардегна, Италия, её отцу по имени Comita III de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Spella, 23. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Boniface of Saluzzo. Она умерла в 1264 г. в местности салуззо, кунео, пиемонт, Италия в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности эббазя санта маря, ривеллоу, кунео, пиемонт, Италия.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Boniface of Saluzzo. Murió en 1264, en Saluzzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultada en Abbazia Santa Maria, Revello, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia.
